# src/generation/prompts

**Prompt template library defining system/user prompts for Claude-powered .sum file analysis and AGENTS.md directory documentation generation, enforcing density rules, behavioral contract preservation, and incremental update semantics.**

## Contents

- [builder.ts](./builder.ts) — prompt orchestration layer assembling file-level (`buildFilePrompt`) and directory-level (`buildDirectoryPrompt`) LLM contexts by injecting source code, related files, import maps, compression ratios, and user notes into template strings from `templates.ts`; selects update vs full-generation system prompts based on `existingSum`/`existingAgentsMd` presence; reads `.sum` files, child AGENTS.md, manifests, and `.annex.sum` references via parallel I/O; detects syntax highlighting language via `detectLanguage` covering 20+ extensions
- [index.ts](./index.ts) — barrel module re-exporting `PromptContext`, `SUMMARY_GUIDELINES`, `buildFilePrompt`, `buildDirectoryPrompt`, `detectLanguage`
- [templates.ts](./templates.ts) — defines five prompt constants (`FILE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `FILE_USER_PROMPT`, `FILE_UPDATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `DIRECTORY_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `DIRECTORY_UPDATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`) encoding Claude's generation rules: mandatory anchor term preservation (exact identifier casing), density requirements (≥1 identifier per sentence, zero filler phrases), behavioral contract retention (verbatim regex patterns/constants/workflow rules), annex overflow patterns for large constants, incremental update preservation semantics (update only changed sections, preserve stable text verbatim), path accuracy mandates (use Import Map paths, never guess), consistency enforcement (no self-contradiction)
- [types.ts](./types.ts) — exports `PromptContext` interface (`filePath`, `content`, optional `contextFiles`/`existingSum`/`compressionRatio`) and `SUMMARY_GUIDELINES` constant defining .sum target length (300-500 chars), inclusion rules (purpose, public interface, patterns, algorithms, behavioral contracts with verbatim artifacts, annex references), exclusion rules (control flow, TODOs, broad architecture)

## Architecture

Two-tier prompt assembly: `builder.ts` orchestrates data gathering (file content, related files, imports, manifests, existing docs) and injects into templates from `templates.ts`; template selection switches between full-generation vs incremental-update system prompts based on existence of prior documentation; `types.ts` defines input schema (`PromptContext`) and output guidelines (`SUMMARY_GUIDELINES`).

## Behavioral Contracts

Manifest detection filenames (builder.ts): `['package.json', 'Cargo.toml', 'go.mod', 'pyproject.toml', 'pom.xml', 'build.gradle', 'Gemfile', 'composer.json', 'CMakeLists.txt', 'Makefile']`

Source file regex for import extraction (builder.ts): `/\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx|py|go|rs|java|kt)$/`

Compression trigger threshold (builder.ts): `compressionRatio < 0.5` injects dynamic size targets (`sourceSize * ratio`, max `target * 1.2`) and ultra-compression techniques (telegraphic style, one-sentence-per-export, abbreviations)

Template placeholder pattern (builder.ts): `/\{\{FILE_PATH\}\}/g`, `/\{\{CONTENT\}\}/g`, `/\{\{LANG\}\}/g` replaced via `.replace()` in user prompts

Language map (builder.ts): covers `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.js`, `.jsx`, `.py`, `.rb`, `.go`, `.rs`, `.java`, `.kt`, `.swift`, `.cs`, `.php`, `.vue`, `.svelte`, `.json`, `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.md`, `.css`, `.scss`, `.html`, defaults to `text`

Annex file detection (builder.ts): entries ending with `.annex.sum` listed as reproduction-critical constants

Generated marker detection (builder.ts): uses `GENERATED_MARKER_PREFIX` from `../writers/agents-md.js` to distinguish user-authored vs auto-generated AGENTS.md

## Reproduction-Critical Constants

Full prompt template text: [templates.annex.sum](./templates.annex.sum)

## Workflow & Conventions

Density enforcement: every sentence must reference ≥1 identifier (function/class/type/const name); prohibited filler phrases include "this file", "this directory", "provides", "responsible for", "is used to"; use telegraphic shorthand ("exports X, Y, Z" not "this module exports a function called X...")

Anchor term preservation: all exported symbols must appear exactly as written in source (preserve casing), missing any export constitutes failure

Behavioral contracts never excluded: regex patterns (verbatim in backticks), format strings, magic constants, prompt templates, error codes, env var names, file format specs

Workflow rules never excluded: contribution requirements (branch naming, commit formats), testing mandates (coverage thresholds, "never delete tests"), tool usage rules (CLI commands, linter configs), approval workflows, AI agent instructions, code conventions (naming standards, import ordering, TypeScript strictness)

Incremental update preservation: when `existingSum` or `existingAgentsMd` present, preserve unchanged sections verbatim (don't rephrase/reorganize), update only code-affected content, maintain behavioral contracts and annex references unless source modified them

Path accuracy (AGENTS.md generation): reference files/modules using only paths from Import Map section, never invent/rename/guess module paths, use exact directory names from Project Directory Structure, omit cross-reference if path uncertain

Output format: start response directly with bold purpose statement, no preamble/thinking/meta-commentary ("Here is...", "Based on my analysis..."), AGENTS.md first line must be exactly `<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer -->`
